categories.description: Verify that all cryptographic modules fail securely, and errors are handled in a way that does not enable Padding Oracle attacks.

categories.name: Algorithms
categories.description: Verify that industry proven or government approved cryptographic algorithms, modes, and libraries are used, instead of custom coded cryptography. ([C8](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Algorithms
categories.description: Verify that encryption initialization vector, cipher configuration, and block modes are configured securely using the latest advice.

categories.name: Algorithms
categories.description: Verify that random number, encryption or hashing algorithms, key lengths, rounds, ciphers or modes, can be reconfigured, upgraded, or swapped at any time, to protect against cryptographic breaks. ([C8](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Algorithms
categories.description: Verify that known insecure block modes (i.e. ECB, etc.), padding modes (i.e. PKCS#1 v1.5, etc.), ciphers with small block sizes (i.e. Triple-DES, Blowfish, etc.), and weak hashing algorithms (i.e. MD5, SHA1, etc.) are not used unless required for backwards compatibility.

categories.name: Algorithms
categories.description: Verify that nonces, initialization vectors, and other single use numbers must not be used more than once with a given encryption key. The method of generation must be appropriate for the algorithm being used.

categories.name: Algorithms
categories.description: Verify that encrypted data is authenticated via signatures, authenticated cipher modes, or HMAC to ensure that ciphertext is not altered by an unauthorized party.

categories.name: Algorithms
categories.description: Verify that all cryptographic operations are constant-time, with no 'short-circuit' operations in comparisons, calculations, or returns, to avoid leaking information.

categories.name: Random Values
categories.description: Verify that all random numbers, random file names, random GUIDs, and random strings are generated using the cryptographic module's approved cryptographically secure random number generator when these random values are intended to be not guessable by an attacker.

categories.name: Random Values
categories.description: Verify that random GUIDs are created using the GUID v4 algorithm, and a Cryptographically-secure Pseudo-random Number Generator (CSPRNG). GUIDs created using other pseudo-random number generators may be predictable.

categories.name: Random Values
categories.description: Verify that random numbers are created with proper entropy even when the application is under heavy load, or that the application degrades gracefully in such circumstances.

categories.name: Secret Management
categories.description: Verify that a secrets management solution such as a key vault is used to securely create, store, control access to and destroy secrets. ([C8](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Secret Management
categories.description: Verify that key material is not exposed to the application but instead uses an isolated security module like a vault for cryptographic operations. ([C8](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Log Content
categories.description: Verify that the application does not log credentials or payment details. Session tokens should only be stored in logs in an irreversible, hashed form. ([C9, C10](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Log Content
categories.description: Verify that the application does not log other sensitive data as defined under local privacy laws or relevant security policy. ([C9](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Log Content
categories.description: Verify that the application logs security relevant events including successful and failed authentication events, access control failures, deserialization failures and input validation failures. ([C5, C7](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Log Content
categories.description: Verify that each log event includes necessary information that would allow for a detailed investigation of the timeline when an event happens. ([C9](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Log Processing
categories.description: Verify that all authentication decisions are logged, without storing sensitive session tokens or passwords. This should include requests with relevant metadata needed for security investigations.

categories.name: Log Processing
categories.description: Verify that all access control decisions can be logged and all failed decisions are logged. This should include requests with relevant metadata needed for security investigations.

categories.name: Log Protection
categories.description: Verify that all logging components appropriately encode data to prevent log injection. ([C9](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Log Protection
categories.description: [DELETED, DUPLICATE OF 7.3.1]

categories.name: Log Protection
categories.description: Verify that security logs are protected from unauthorized access and modification. ([C9](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Log Protection
categories.description: Verify that time sources are synchronized to the correct time and time zone. Strongly consider logging only in UTC if systems are global to assist with post-incident forensic analysis. ([C9](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Error Handling
categories.description: Verify that a generic message is shown when an unexpected or security sensitive error occurs, potentially with a unique ID which support personnel can use to investigate. ([C10](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Error Handling
categories.description: Verify that exception handling (or a functional equivalent) is used across the codebase to account for expected and unexpected error conditions. ([C10](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Error Handling
categories.description: Verify that a "last resort" error handler is defined which will catch all unhandled exceptions. ([C10](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: General Data Protection
categories.description: Verify the application protects sensitive data from being cached in server components such as load balancers and application caches.

categories.name: General Data Protection
categories.description: Verify that all cached or temporary copies of sensitive data stored on the server are protected from unauthorized access or purged/invalidated after the authorized user accesses the sensitive data.

categories.name: General Data Protection
categories.description: Verify the application minimizes the number of parameters in a request, such as hidden fields, Ajax variables, cookies and header values.

categories.name: General Data Protection
categories.description: Verify the application can detect and alert on abnormal numbers of requests, such as by IP, user, total per hour or day, or whatever makes sense for the application.

categories.name: General Data Protection
categories.description: Verify that regular backups of important data are performed and that test restoration of data is performed.

categories.name: General Data Protection
categories.description: Verify that backups are stored securely to prevent data from being stolen or corrupted.

categories.name: Client-side Data Protection
categories.description: Verify the application sets sufficient anti-caching headers so that sensitive data is not cached in modern browsers.

categories.name: Client-side Data Protection
categories.description: Verify that data stored in browser storage (such as localStorage, sessionStorage, IndexedDB, or cookies) does not contain sensitive data.

categories.name: Client-side Data Protection
categories.description: Verify that authenticated data is cleared from client storage, such as the browser DOM, after the client or session is terminated.

categories.name: Sensitive Private Data
categories.description: Verify that sensitive data is sent to the server in the HTTP message body or headers, and that query string parameters from any HTTP verb do not contain sensitive data.

categories.name: Sensitive Private Data
categories.description: Verify that users have a method to remove or export their data on demand.

categories.name: Sensitive Private Data
categories.description: Verify that users are provided clear language regarding collection and use of supplied personal information and that users have provided opt-in consent for the use of that data before it is used in any way.

categories.name: Sensitive Private Data
categories.description: Verify that all sensitive data created and processed by the application has been identified, and ensure that a policy is in place on how to deal with sensitive data. ([C8](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Sensitive Private Data
categories.description: Verify accessing sensitive data is audited (without logging the sensitive data itself), if the data is collected under relevant data protection directives or where logging of access is required.

categories.name: Sensitive Private Data
categories.description: Verify that sensitive information contained in memory is overwritten as soon as it is no longer required to mitigate memory dumping attacks, using zeroes or random data.

categories.name: Sensitive Private Data
categories.description: Verify that sensitive or private information that is required to be encrypted, is encrypted using approved algorithms that provide both confidentiality and integrity. ([C8](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Sensitive Private Data
categories.description: Verify that sensitive personal information is subject to data retention classification, such that old or out of date data is deleted automatically, on a schedule, or as the situation requires.

categories.name: Client Communication Security
categories.description: Verify that TLS is used for all client connectivity, and does not fall back to insecure or unencrypted communications. ([C8](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Client Communication Security
categories.description: Verify using up to date TLS testing tools that only strong cipher suites are enabled, with the strongest cipher suites set as preferred.

categories.name: Client Communication Security
categories.description: Verify that only the latest recommended versions of the TLS protocol are enabled, such as TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3. The latest version of the TLS protocol should be the preferred option.

categories.name: Server Communication Security
categories.description: Verify that connections to and from the server use trusted TLS certificates. Where internally generated or self-signed certificates are used, the server must be configured to only trust specific internal CAs and specific self-signed certificates. All others should be rejected.

categories.name: Server Communication Security
categories.description: Verify that encrypted communications such as TLS is used for all inbound and outbound connections, including for management ports, monitoring, authentication, API, or web service calls, database, cloud, serverless, mainframe, external, and partner connections. The server must not fall back to insecure or unencrypted protocols.

categories.name: Server Communication Security
categories.description: Verify that all encrypted connections to external systems that involve sensitive information or functions are authenticated.

categories.name: Server Communication Security
categories.description: Verify that proper certification revocation, such as Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) Stapling, is enabled and configured.

categories.name: Server Communication Security
categories.description: Verify that backend TLS connection failures are logged.

categories.name: Code Integrity
categories.description: Verify that a code analysis tool is in use that can detect potentially malicious code, such as time functions, unsafe file operations and network connections.

categories.name: Malicious Code Search
categories.description: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain unauthorized phone home or data collection capabilities. Where such functionality exists, obtain the user's permission for it to operate before collecting any data.

categories.name: Malicious Code Search
categories.description: Verify that the application does not ask for unnecessary or excessive permissions to privacy related features or sensors, such as contacts, cameras, microphones, or location.

categories.name: Malicious Code Search
categories.description: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain back doors, such as hard-coded or additional undocumented accounts or keys, code obfuscation, undocumented binary blobs, rootkits, or anti-debugging, insecure debugging features, or otherwise out of date, insecure, or hidden functionality that could be used maliciously if discovered.

categories.name: Malicious Code Search
categories.description: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain time bombs by searching for date and time related functions.

categories.name: Malicious Code Search
categories.description: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain malicious code, such as salami attacks, logic bypasses, or logic bombs.

categories.name: Malicious Code Search
categories.description: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain Easter eggs or any other potentially unwanted functionality.

categories.name: Application Integrity
categories.description: Verify that if the application has a client or server auto-update feature, updates should be obtained over secure channels and digitally signed. The update code must validate the digital signature of the update before installing or executing the update.

categories.name: Application Integrity
categories.description: Verify that the application employs integrity protections, such as code signing or subresource integrity. The application must not load or execute code from untrusted sources, such as loading includes, modules, plugins, code, or libraries from untrusted sources or the Internet.

categories.name: Application Integrity
categories.description: Verify that the application has protection from subdomain takeovers if the application relies upon DNS entries or DNS subdomains, such as expired domain names, out of date DNS pointers or CNAMEs, expired projects at public source code repos, or transient cloud APIs, serverless functions, or storage buckets (*autogen-bucket-id*.cloud.example.com) or similar. Protections can include ensuring that DNS names used by applications are regularly checked for expiry or change.

categories.name: Business Logic Security
categories.description: Verify that the application will only process business logic flows for the same user in sequential step order and without skipping steps.

categories.name: Business Logic Security
categories.description: Verify that the application will only process business logic flows with all steps being processed in realistic human time, i.e. transactions are not submitted too quickly.

categories.name: Business Logic Security
categories.description: Verify the application has appropriate limits for specific business actions or transactions which are correctly enforced on a per user basis.

categories.name: Business Logic Security
categories.description: Verify that the application has anti-automation controls to protect against excessive calls such as mass data exfiltration, business logic requests, file uploads or denial of service attacks.

categories.name: Business Logic Security
categories.description: Verify the application has business logic limits or validation to protect against likely business risks or threats, identified using threat modeling or similar methodologies.

categories.name: Business Logic Security
categories.description: Verify that the application does not suffer from "Time Of Check to Time Of Use" (TOCTOU) issues or other race conditions for sensitive operations.

categories.name: Business Logic Security
categories.description: Verify that the application monitors for unusual events or activity from a business logic perspective. For example, attempts to perform actions out of order or actions which a normal user would never attempt. ([C9](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Business Logic Security
categories.description: Verify that the application has configurable alerting when automated attacks or unusual activity is detected.

categories.name: File Upload
categories.description: Verify that the application will not accept large files that could fill up storage or cause a denial of service.

categories.name: File Upload
categories.description: Verify that the application checks compressed files (e.g. zip, gz, docx, odt) against maximum allowed uncompressed size and against maximum number of files before uncompressing the file.

categories.name: File Upload
categories.description: Verify that a file size quota and maximum number of files per user is enforced to ensure that a single user cannot fill up the storage with too many files, or excessively large files.

categories.name: File Integrity
categories.description: Verify that files obtained from untrusted sources are validated to be of expected type based on the file's content.

categories.name: File Execution
categories.description: Verify that user-submitted filename metadata is not used directly by system or framework filesystems and that a URL API is used to protect against path traversal.

categories.name: File Execution
categories.description: Verify that user-submitted filename metadata is validated or ignored to prevent the disclosure, creation, updating or removal of local files (LFI).

categories.name: File Execution
categories.description: Verify that user-submitted filename metadata is validated or ignored to prevent the disclosure or execution of remote files via Remote File Inclusion (RFI) or Server-side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks.

categories.name: File Execution
categories.description: Verify that the application protects against Reflective File Download (RFD) by validating or ignoring user-submitted filenames in a JSON, JSONP, or URL parameter, the response Content-Type header should be set to text/plain, and the Content-Disposition header should have a fixed filename.

categories.name: File Execution
categories.description: Verify that untrusted file metadata is not used directly with system API or libraries, to protect against OS command injection.

categories.name: File Execution
categories.description: Verify that the application does not include and execute functionality from untrusted sources, such as unverified content distribution networks, JavaScript libraries, node npm libraries, or server-side DLLs.

categories.name: File Storage
categories.description: Verify that files obtained from untrusted sources are stored outside the web root, with limited permissions.

categories.name: File Storage
categories.description: Verify that files obtained from untrusted sources are scanned by antivirus scanners to prevent upload and serving of known malicious content.

categories.name: File Download
categories.description: Verify that the web tier is configured to serve only files with specific file extensions to prevent unintentional information and source code leakage. For example, backup files (e.g. .bak), temporary working files (e.g. .swp), compressed files (.zip, .tar.gz, etc) and other extensions commonly used by editors should be blocked unless required.

categories.name: File Download
categories.description: Verify that direct requests to uploaded files will never be executed as HTML/JavaScript content.

categories.name: SSRF Protection
categories.description: Verify that the web or application server is configured with an allow list of resources or systems to which the server can send requests or load data/files from.

categories.name: Generic Web Service Security
categories.description: Verify that all application components use the same encodings and parsers to avoid parsing attacks that exploit different URI or file parsing behavior that could be used in SSRF and RFI attacks.

categories.name: Generic Web Service Security
categories.description: [DELETED, DUPLICATE OF 4.3.1]

categories.name: Generic Web Service Security
categories.description: Verify API URLs do not expose sensitive information, such as the API key, session tokens etc.

categories.name: Generic Web Service Security
categories.description: Verify that authorization decisions are made at both the URI, enforced by programmatic or declarative security at the controller or router, and at the resource level, enforced by model-based permissions.

categories.name: Generic Web Service Security
categories.description: Verify that requests containing unexpected or missing content types are rejected with appropriate headers (HTTP response status 406 Unacceptable or 415 Unsupported Media Type).

categories.name: RESTful Web Service
categories.description: Verify that enabled RESTful HTTP methods are a valid choice for the user or action, such as preventing normal users using DELETE or PUT on protected API or resources.

categories.name: RESTful Web Service
categories.description: Verify that JSON schema validation is in place and verified before accepting input.

categories.name: RESTful Web Service
categories.description: Verify that RESTful web services that utilize cookies are protected from Cross-Site Request Forgery via the use of at least one or more of the following: double submit cookie pattern, CSRF nonces, or Origin request header checks.

categories.name: RESTful Web Service
categories.description: [DELETED, DUPLICATE OF 11.1.4]

categories.name: RESTful Web Service
categories.description: Verify that REST services explicitly check the incoming Content-Type to be the expected one, such as application/xml or application/json.

categories.name: RESTful Web Service
categories.description: Verify that the message headers and payload are trustworthy and not modified in transit. Requiring strong encryption for transport (TLS only) may be sufficient in many cases as it provides both confidentiality and integrity protection. Per-message digital signatures can provide additional assurance on top of the transport protections for high-security applications but bring with them additional complexity and risks to weigh against the benefits.

categories.name: SOAP Web Service
categories.description: Verify that XSD schema validation takes place to ensure a properly formed XML document, followed by validation of each input field before any processing of that data takes place.

categories.name: SOAP Web Service
categories.description: Verify that the message payload is signed using WS-Security to ensure reliable transport between client and service.

categories.name: GraphQL
categories.description: Verify that a query allow list or a combination of depth limiting and amount limiting is used to prevent GraphQL or data layer expression Denial of Service (DoS) as a result of expensive, nested queries. For more advanced scenarios, query cost analysis should be used.

categories.name: GraphQL
categories.description: Verify that GraphQL or other data layer authorization logic should be implemented at the business logic layer instead of the GraphQL layer.

categories.name: Build and Deploy
categories.description: Verify that the application build and deployment processes are performed in a secure and repeatable way, such as CI / CD automation, automated configuration management, and automated deployment scripts.

categories.name: Build and Deploy
categories.description: Verify that compiler flags are configured to enable all available buffer overflow protections and warnings, including stack randomization, data execution prevention, and to break the build if an unsafe pointer, memory, format string, integer, or string operations are found.

categories.name: Build and Deploy
categories.description: Verify that server configuration is hardened as per the recommendations of the application server and frameworks in use.

categories.name: Build and Deploy
categories.description: Verify that the application, configuration, and all dependencies can be re-deployed using automated deployment scripts, built from a documented and tested runbook in a reasonable time, or restored from backups in a timely fashion.

categories.name: Build and Deploy
categories.description: Verify that authorized administrators can verify the integrity of all security-relevant configurations to detect tampering.

categories.name: Dependency
categories.description: Verify that all components are up to date, preferably using a dependency checker during build or compile time. ([C2](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Dependency
categories.description: Verify that all unneeded features, documentation, sample applications and configurations are removed.

categories.name: Dependency
categories.description: Verify that if application assets, such as JavaScript libraries, CSS or web fonts, are hosted externally on a Content Delivery Network (CDN) or external provider, Subresource Integrity (SRI) is used to validate the integrity of the asset.

categories.name: Dependency
categories.description: Verify that third party components come from pre-defined, trusted and continually maintained repositories. ([C2](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Dependency
categories.description: Verify that a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) is maintained of all third party libraries in use. ([C2](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Dependency
categories.description: Verify that the attack surface is reduced by sandboxing or encapsulating third party libraries to expose only the required behaviour into the application. ([C2](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

categories.name: Unintended Security Disclosure
categories.description: [DELETED, DUPLICATE OF 7.4.1]

categories.name: Unintended Security Disclosure
categories.description: Verify that web or application server and application framework debug modes are disabled in production to eliminate debug features, developer consoles, and unintended security disclosures.

categories.name: Unintended Security Disclosure
categories.description: Verify that the HTTP headers or any part of the HTTP response do not expose detailed version information of system components.

categories.name: HTTP Security Headers
categories.description: Verify that every HTTP response contains a Content-Type header. Also specify a safe character set (e.g., UTF-8, ISO-8859-1) if the content types are text/*, /+xml and application/xml. Content must match with the provided Content-Type header.

categories.name: HTTP Security Headers
categories.description: Verify that all API responses contain a Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="api.json" header (or other appropriate filename for the content type).

categories.name: HTTP Security Headers
categories.description: Verify that a Content Security Policy (CSP) response header is in place that helps mitigate impact for XSS attacks like HTML, DOM, JSON, and JavaScript injection vulnerabilities.

categories.name: HTTP Security Headers
categories.description: Verify that all responses contain a X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header.

categories.name: HTTP Security Headers
categories.description: Verify that a Strict-Transport-Security header is included on all responses and for all subdomains, such as Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=15724800; includeSubdomains.

categories.name: HTTP Security Headers
categories.description: Verify that a suitable Referrer-Policy header is included to avoid exposing sensitive information in the URL through the Referer header to untrusted parties.

categories.name: HTTP Security Headers
categories.description: Verify that the content of a web application cannot be embedded in a third-party site by default and that embedding of the exact resources is only allowed where necessary by using suitable Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors and X-Frame-Options response headers.

categories.name: HTTP Request Header Validation
categories.description: Verify that the application server only accepts the HTTP methods in use by the application/API, including pre-flight OPTIONS, and logs/alerts on any requests that are not valid for the application context.

categories.name: HTTP Request Header Validation
categories.description: Verify that the supplied Origin header is not used for authentication or access control decisions, as the Origin header can easily be changed by an attacker.

categories.name: HTTP Request Header Validation
categories.description: Verify that the Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) Access-Control-Allow-Origin header uses a strict allow list of trusted domains and subdomains to match against and does not support the "null" origin.

categories.name: HTTP Request Header Validation
categories.description: Verify that HTTP headers added by a trusted proxy or SSO devices, such as a bearer token, are authenticated by the application.
